text: Highlight Clip Adam Back: Is Satoshi's Electronic Cash Vision Dead? On June 2, 2026, at the Proof of Talk conference, Bitcoin OG Adam Back believes that the evolutionary paths of retail adoption and asset investment coexist. He states that in emerging markets with high inflation, Bitcoin is still widely used for retail payments and remittances, whereas in the developed world, it is more commonly treated as an investment asset. Enabling the public to gain financial benefits through structured products like ETFs is an inevitable part of the adoption process. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: CoinShares: Professional Bitcoin Holdings Fell to 261K BTC in Q1, With Hedge Funds and Brokers Driving 95% of the Reduction According to CoinShares' 13F analysis, professional investors' Bitcoin holdings fell from 313K BTC to 261K BTC in Q1 2026, a 17% quarter-on-quarter decline. 13F filers reduced their exposure by about 52.5K BTC, with hedge funds and brokerages accounting for roughly 95% of the reduction; hedge fund holdings fell 39%, while broker holdings declined 53%. By contrast, banks increased their total holdings to 15.2K BTC, with JPMorgan and Wells Fargo adding exposure and Citi disclosing its first BTC position. — link

text: RoboStrategy, Inc. to Host Virtual Investor Presentation on June 10, 2026 RoboStrategy a Nasdaq-listed, closed-end investment company giving public-market investors access to a portfolio of private robotics companies • "Private robotics investments, public company" • A closed-end fund has two prices: NAV per share and market price. When demand pushes market price above NAV, it trades at a premium • Issuing new shares above NAV is accretive - new buyers pay in more cash per share than the NAV claim they take, lifting NAV/share for everyone • 10% new shares at 3× premium for 5 rounds → +131% NAV/share; at 5× premium over 10 cycles → +2,118%; at parity, flat • "Two engines": portfolio returns + premium harvesting. They explicitly call issuance "the entry point, not the strategy" - raise high, deploy into private robotics, scale, repeat Links • Press release • Presentation slide deck

text: The Four Ideologies of Bitcoin 4 emerging ideologies in the Bitcoin community as Bitcoin evolves into a global monetary network Maximalists view Bitcoin as the supreme decentralized money solving scarcity and debasement; Capitalists emphasize integration into economies via companies, banks, and credit; Technologists focus on protocol improvements for scalability and security; Fundamentalists prioritize protecting core principles like self-custody and immutability Saylor argues these views are complementary rather than conflicting, advocating a synthesis that keeps the base layer sacred while enabling disciplined expansion through higher layers and institutional adoption

text: Michael Saylor Breaks Bitcoin Community Into Four Camps, Warns Against Extremes Strategy Chairman Michael Saylor published a framework describing four major Bitcoin ideologies: Maximalists, who view Bitcoin as the dominant monetary network; Capitalists, who focus on integrating Bitcoin into global finance; Technologists, who advocate protocol improvements; and Fundamentalists, who prioritize self-custody, decentralization and immutability. Saylor argued Bitcoin's future depends on balancing conviction, adoption, innovation and preservation, calling for "disciplined expansion" while keeping the base layer largely unchanged. — link

text: The ZCash Orchard bug What is Orchard • Bug found in Zcash's newest privacy feature called Orchard (their newest, most private "shielded" pool) • Users deposit normal ZEC into Orchard → you get private "notes" which can be spent privately, then withdraw back to transparent ZEC later • Orchard is basically a big shared vault of real ZEC. Your private notes are just claims on whatever ZEC is actually sitting in that vault The bug • If someone had exploited the bug, they could create fake private notes out of thin air • Since exploiters also have "fake" notes too, when everyone tries to withdraw, there isn't enough real ZEC for all the claims • Exactly like the KelpDAO hack: fake deposit receipts competing for the real money in the vault • The vault could become insolvent (more claims than real coins) • Important clarification: this does NOT let anyone mint unlimited ZEC across the whole blockchain • It only messes up the Orchard pool itself. Total ZEC supply stays safe The "Turnstile" saves the day • Zcash has a clever safety gate called the turnstile • It does public accounting: only lets you withdraw as much ZEC as has publicly entered the Orchard pool • You literally cannot withdraw more than the vault should have • The bug can't drain the rest of the chain • Damage is contained to the Orchard pool only Mitigation • The old Orchard pool might have hidden fake notes • Plan is everyone moves their ZEC out of the old pool (unshield) • Launch a brand new Orchard pool that starts at zero • The new pool only accepts deposits under the fixed rules Reccomended action: unshield from Orchard right now - to avoid any potential bank-run panic and let the team cleanly reboot the shielded pool

text: Got stopped out. Shouldn't have been greedy and taken the 3% move I caught in just a few hours since I bottom ticked it. Liquidation cascades always go deeper than you usually expect and I should have had better process and screen time to realize this. Overall been disappointed in my lack of discipline for trading and life in general these past several months. I've felt I became very unstructured in my day to day life which has trickled over dramatically in my professionalism towards trading and making money. It's one thing to tell yourself to have a month break and go enjoy life/party/time away from the markets. It's another thing when you tell yourself you're going to lock in and then do these activities anyway. I'm perfectly happy to step away from markets when I feel I'm burnt out or have minimal interests in the way specific assets are trading. Although when you tell yourself you're going to lock in and then aren't putting in the necessary screen time, not only are you not moving towards your goals, but when the activities keeping you away from the markets are counter productive like partying, you're not only sitting still but moving further away from your goals. This is one of the most psychologically crushing aspects for my mindset when I tell myself I'm going to do something and I don't do it. It eats away at my core knowing I'm not being honest with myself and staying disciplined to my own word. Becoming a professional trader is about understanding your psyche to the deepest degree. Going against your discipline and word with yourself is putting cracks in the very foundation you stand on as a trader. You begin subconsciously poking holes in your framework and edge as you're showing yourself that you aren't serious, that you don't really care about the quality of process and dedication to one of the most competitive industries in the world. It's like a body builder eating pancakes and ice cream while preparing for Mr Olympia. This lack of discipline has been a double edged sword for myself as I have the exact formula needed to make a comeback and become in tune with the market again. For those who've been in this channel for a while you'll know how extensively I've spoken about becoming aware of your energy levels and building process and risk management regarding my read on the market. My method has worked countless times and it's tailored specifically to how my mind and body responds to my trading style. This has caused over confidence in the fact that I don't need to work as hard as I'm very talented and can manifest my desires through hard work quite easily. Which is a bad trap to fall into due to the fact that the more times I successfully do it, the less stress I have, except that stress is what makes me outperform in the first place. The past several months has been a tug of war with my psyche in the sense that I have all the answers but the lack of will power to execute. I'll have my foot on the gas for one month then the next month I become sloppy and stop putting the screen time, wondering why I'm not compounding results. Based on previous instances, I normally need around one month of warming up, second month where I'm feeling quite confident and the third month is where I crank screen time to the max, where majority of my gains usually come from before repeating the cycle again. Instead I've been slowly dying by death of a thousand paper cuts and letting ego take the wheel. My focus moving forward is to turn the ship around by falling in love with the process again and letting my previous guidelines and frameworks abstract any chance of my ego and emotions overruling my decisions. Sometimes obsession alone isn't enough and you need to draw from toxic emotions like anger to truly spark the necessary change required.

text: According to Binance market data, ZEC is trading at $272.79, down 48.4% over the past 24 hours. text: According to ChosunBiz, South Korean police are investigating local Polymarket users on suspicion of illegal gambling, reportedly the first such case involving domestic users. The probe is led by Gangwon Provincial Police at the request of the National Police Agency. Under South Korean law, betting on platforms other than Sports Toto is illegal, and users may face fines of up to 10 million won under Article 246 of the Criminal Act. ChosunBiz said Polymarket is accessible without IP circumvention, and its markets on South Korea's June 3 local elections saw betting volume reach tens of billions of won. — link

text: Correction: Largest SOL Treasury Company Forward Industries Transfers 455k SOL to Coinbase Prime Forward Industries has deposited 455,784 SOL (worth approx. $31.87M) to Coinbase Prime after a month of wallet inactivity. Similar large transfers to Coinbase Prime in the past were later confirmed as custody/staking-related moves and did not result in actual reduction of holdings. Since launching its Solana treasury strategy in Sep 2025, the company has acquired ~6.83M SOL at an average cost of $232.08. It currently holds around 7M SOL valued at ~$458M, with an unrealized loss of ~$1.13B. — link

text: This episode of the Wu Blockchain podcast features GMTrade co-founder William, covering its evolution from a GMX Solana branch into an independent protocol, pooled trading model, Chainlink oracle integration, LP yield sources, and market expansion. William said GMTrade is a Solana-based decentralized perpetuals protocol with nearly 90 markets, including forex, commodities and indices, and plans to expand further into real-world assets. He said GMTrade focuses on open interest, while LP yield mainly comes from trading fees, borrowing fees and liquidation fees. — link

text: Zooko detailed a critical counterfeiting vulnerability in Zcash's Orchard shielded pool, discovered on May 29, 2026 by security researcher Taylor Hornby using Anthropic's Opus 4.8 AI during targeted audits; it stemmed from an under-constrained elliptic curve multiplication allowing unlimited undetectable fake ZEC and was present since the pool's 2022 activation The flaw was rapidly disclosed to ZODL and fixed via emergency response, though Orchard's privacy features prevent cryptographic proof of prior exploitation

text: Binance Research said crypto exchanges are becoming a new gateway to global equity markets, with nearly 93% of Binance stock trading users coming from emerging markets. The report estimates that by 2031, crypto exchanges could channel $2 trillion in incremental capital and nearly 300 million new investors into global equity markets. In a bull case, crypto users could bring $5 trillion in annual incremental equity capital over the next five years. It added that stablecoin-settled stock trading and tokenized equities could reduce cross-border costs, while TradFi-linked perpetuals already account for about 10% of stablecoin trading volume. — link

text: Highlight Clip Journalist: Flaunting Bitcoin makes you a target for Wrench Attacks On May 22, 2026, bitcoin journalist Joe Nakamoto advised crypto investors in his video to stop talking about Bitcoin and avoid flexing their assets on social media or at dinner parties. He states that this crisis is not unique to France, and publicly discussing crypto assets easily makes one a target for physical wrench attacks. However, he believes the existence of such attacks ironically proves Bitcoin's true value, directly refuting ECB President Christine Lagarde's remark that crypto assets have "no intrinsic value." Finally, he states that crypto users do not need to live in fear and should actively organize defenses, improve security setups, and reduce exposure. — link
